message:
perf(page-cache): replace flat-cache tombstone counter with boolean hint

FlatPageSlots previously tracked exact tombstone counts via an
AtomicUsize so that clear() could short-circuit when both resident and
tombstone counts were zero. Maintaining the counter required an extra
fetch_update on every reclaim-through-tombstone insert and left the
`decrement_tombstones_after_reclaim` helper as a dedicated branch in
the hot insert path. Worse, that counter could momentarily undercount
tombstones under concurrent evictions, so the short-circuit wasn't
strictly safe — it was correct only because the other checks in
clear() compensated.

Collapse the counter into a single AtomicBool (`has_tombstones`) that
acts as a conservative "tombstones may be present" hint:

- Every eviction path flips the flag to true with a Release store.
- clear() swaps it back to false with AcqRel; if the swap sees false
  and the resident count is zero, the early-return short-circuit
  still fires, with the only downside being that a pure-tombstone
  clear() may do one extra Acquire load's worth of work.
- Removes `decrement_tombstones_after_reclaim` entirely; reclaiming
  a tombstone slot no longer needs to decrement anything.
- Debug prints `has_tombstones: bool` instead of the stale counter
  field.

Test updates mirror the contract change: the existing tombstone test
in FlatPageSlots::tests now asserts the flag is true after a forced
drain and false after clear(), with comments describing the new
"cleanup hint" semantics (note: the clear→false assertion is valid
only when no eviction races clear, matching the hint's looser
guarantee).

Net effect: one less RMW per insert-over-tombstone, no more
fetch_update path on the insert hot path, and the short-circuit
semantics become intentional (hint, not counter) instead of
accidental (race-vulnerable counter).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
